page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.cm4.2xlarge is a general-purpose virtual machine instance powered by the KVM hypervisor. It features an Intel Xeon E5-2667 v4 processor with an x86_64 architecture, operating at a clock speed of 3.2 GHz. The instance provides 8 dedicated physical cores with 1 thread per core, resulting in 8 vCPUs. It is equipped with 32.0 GB of system memory, yielding a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core. This instance does not include local storage or GPU accelerators, and it offers a baseline network bandwidth of 3 Gbps.

Performance benchmarks indicate average capabilities across standard operations. The instance achieves average ratings in memory bandwidth read-only tests, with 414.69 GB/sec for cached small blocks and 39.89 GB/sec for uncached large blocks. However, memory write bandwidth for large blocks is weak at 23.56 GB/sec. CPU performance is mixed; while Passmark CPU and Geekbench single-core scores fall into the average tier, stress-ng single-core and multi-core CPU performance, along with Geekbench multi-core scores, are positioned in the bottom 25%. Cryptographic and physics processing are also weak, though database operations and Redis SET operations maintain average throughput.

Given the dedicated CPU allocation and balanced memory-to-vCPU ratio, the ecs.cm4.2xlarge is suitable for moderate general-purpose workloads. These include static web serving, where it achieves an average throughput of 7.11 GB/sec, as well as software compilation and basic text or image processing. It is not recommended for intensive multi-threaded computing, large-scale database hosting, or modern machine learning tasks, as demonstrated by its poor performance on medium-sized LLM inference models. The absence of local storage requires external block storage attachment for persistent data.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.cm4.2xlarge is a general-purpose virtual machine instance configured with 8 dedicated vCPUs and 32.0 GB of RAM, running on an Intel Xeon E5-2667 v4 processor at 3.2 GHz. Utilizing the KVM hypervisor, this x86_64 instance provides a balanced 4.0 GB of memory per core but lacks local storage and GPU accelerators. Network performance is anchored by a baseline bandwidth of 3 Gbps. Benchmark data shows average performance in memory read bandwidth, static web serving, and Redis operations, though multi-core CPU scaling and memory write speeds are relatively weak. The instance is best suited for moderate workloads such as static web serving, software compilation, and light database operations, while intensive multi-threaded computing and large-scale LLM inference are constrained by its hardware profile.
og_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.cm4.2xlarge is an x86_64 general-purpose instance featuring 8 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 32 GB RAM, and 3 Gbps network bandwidth, suitable for moderate web serving and compilation.

tagline: A dedicated eight-core x86_64 virtual machine with balanced memory allocation for standard web serving and moderate general-purpose workloads.
bullet_points: Intel Xeon E5-2667 v4 processor with 8 dedicated physical cores and vCPUs
32.0 GB of system memory providing a balanced 4.0 GB per core
Baseline network bandwidth of 3 Gbps with no bundled local storage
Average performance in static web serving and Redis SET operations
Suitable for software compilation, text processing, and light database tasks
